posts.author: AlfaCZE
posts.body: Predictions: 10 of 10 Pending | Tied for 1st with 190 others I like this "Czechia vs Sweden" pattern here lol. Swedish expansion would be pretty good move from Oktagon i think. The talent is sure there, yet the concurence is not so strong as in Poland or England. All over nordic countries are good move imo.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Predictions: 10 of 10 Pending | Tied for 1st with 190 others Szabová vs. FernandesLucia Szabová, SUB, R3Szabova is really good. I was worried she would be cooked post pregnancy but she’s been looking dominant. Her last win was super impressiveHumburger vs. KadestamDominik Humburger, TKO, R1This is such a banger. Kadestam is a really fun signing for Oktagon but sadly he’s not taking things seriously anymore. He got iced in ONE after sleeping Soldic and has looked like a shell of himself. His fight against Aung La was one of the most blatantly fixed fights I’ve ever seen; legitimately puts the Dulgarian fight to shame. He came out sparring with Aung La the entire fight, and then went down to some soft punches in r2. Then he came back in a Muay Thai bout and lost to a 1-4 guy (I heard he looked horrendous). Humberger is a crazy brawler with a. Ton of power. He’s coming off of one of the best fights of 2025 in an all out war with enzigek. This would’ve been an all time fight back in 2022/3 but kadestam is jobbing now. Humburger is gonna viciously ko himLengál vs. AslanerOzan Aslaner, DECLengal is just like the last two guys; great striker with negative grappling. He brawls with everyone and has had some of the most insane fights I’ve ever seen in his oktagon run. Aslaner has a terrible record but he’s pretty underrated imo. He is a bit of a sniffer but he knows what he’s good at and can wrestle his ass off. The judges hate this style too, as he’s gotten two splits he clearly won. Kind of a risky pick since he can’t strike but lengal’s grappling is too bad for me to ignoreRoušal vs. BarkSamuel Bark, DECThis is a banger, two strikers with negative grappling. Bark is one of my fav regional guys to watch, his grappling is awful but he’s a filthy striker. He was a credentialed Muay Thai fighter and has a lethal clinch in MMA. Rousal is pretty underrated but he’s like a worse bark. Funny enough I actually think bark could grapple here and have success. I think he’s the better
